reason: Existing process-local device id cache; value is derived from stable local environment and not used to distinguish main vs sub-agent boundaries.
Existing best-effort warning de-duplication for unavailable pi internals; duplicate warnings across jiti instances are noisy but not a boundary safety issue.
Existing one-shot migration guard for user-global sidecar layout; repeated execution is idempotent and unrelated to sub-agent session classification.
Existing per-process migration de-duplication keyed by path; protects repeated filesystem migration work and is idempotent if split by jiti.
Existing per-process .gitignore de-duplication keyed by project root; repeated ensure calls are idempotent and safe under module splitting.
Existing anonymous session key helper for contexts without persisted ids; WeakMap lifetime follows object identity and does not carry authority across sessions.
Existing companion counter for anonymousSessionIds; only creates diagnostic local labels and does not decide sub-agent/main-session trust.
Existing rule scan cache used by several consumers of rule-injector APIs; cache misses fall back to filesystem scan and do not create new writes by themselves.
Existing rule-injector self-heal scheduler hook shared with abrain startup wiring; split instances degrade scheduling but do not bypass sub-agent guards.
Existing debounce timestamp for rule-injector self-heal scheduling; duplicate debounce state can over-schedule but not pollute sub-agent boundaries.
Existing secret resolution command cache for embedding provider setup; values are configuration lookups and not session-bound mutable user signal.
Existing one-shot warning for legacy forgetting settings; duplicate warnings across loaders are harmless and not a persistence authority boundary.
